quizTitle: Tools and Usage in WISP Installations

questions.questionText: What is the primary use of a crimping tool in WISP installations?
options: To cut holes in walls for cable entry
To strip insulation from power cables
To attach RJ45 connectors to Ethernet cables

questions.questionText: Which tool is used to align a point-to-point antenna during installation?
What does a cable tester do in the context of WISP installation?
options: Checks continuity and pin configuration of network cables
Boosts wireless signal for long-range coverage

questions.questionText: Why is a ladder considered an essential tool for WISP technicians?
options: To access rooftops for antenna installation
To hang Ethernet cables from ceilings

questions.questionText: What tool is commonly used to strip Ethernet cables cleanly without damaging the internal wires?
Which handheld tool is best for creating secure terminations for RJ45 connectors?
What is a POE adaptor used for in WISP installations?
options: To deliver power and data through the same Ethernet cable
To secure cables along a rooftop

questions.questionText: Why might a technician use a level during a WISP setup?
options: To ensure antenna mounts and cable runs are straight and aligned
To power POE devices during testing

questions.questionText: Which of the following tools would most likely be used to secure outdoor cabling in place?
What tool is typically used to drill through concrete or brick walls during cable routing?
